creator: _jkrow
caption: Still not over all the penguin sightings. 🥹🎥 @_jkrow, T+L contributor. 📍@linbladexp. #traveltips #adventuretravel #wildlife

transcription.text: Did you know there's an island where penguins outnumber humans 40,000 to 1? This is South Georgia Island, a remote outpost in the Southern Ocean, north of the Antarctic Peninsula, and one of the most wildlife-dense places on Earth. We arrived by expedition ship en route to Antarctica with Nat Geo Lindblad, and before we were allowed on land, everyone was required to scrub boots, gear, and outerwear as part of strict biosecurity protocols designed to protect the ecosystem. Because once you land, there's no separation between you and the wildlife. Millions of king penguins cover the beaches, elephant seals the size of small cars crowd the shoreline, and in some places, the sand itself seems to move because there are so many animals packed together. In the middle of it all sits Griftikin, a former whaling station from the early 1900s and the final resting place of Antarctic explorer Ernest Shackleton. There's no permanent population here, it's just a small group of seasonal researchers and a tiny post office where you can send a postcard from one of the most remote islands in the Southern Ocean. Whether we actually receive these postcards still remains to be seen.
